text:
In a quiet town nestled between whispering woods and gentle hills lived a tenacious old cat named Gruffelda. Every afternoon, she would patrol the neighborhood garden, teaching young chipmunks how to hunt with expert patience. She was well known for her love of garlic bread treats.

One serene afternoon, while Gruffelda was sunning herself in a favorite nook under an elm tree, she heard a faint thumping sound. Curious and agile, she padded toward the origin of the noise—a small hole under a pile of stones. From there, a tiny squirrel chipped away at something. Slowly, Gruffelda peered down to discover a puzzle—a jigsaw puzzle of her own paw tracks!

Gruffelda pondered this odd find for a moment, her whiskers twitching with interest. Then she flipped the pieces around, carefully arranging them. One by one, the picture began to take shape—a map, perhaps? She traced her paws with her nose across it
